## 🇮🇳 India

### RBI Holds Repo Rate at 5.25% as Growth Slows to 6.9% Projection

The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee unanimously voted to keep the repo rate at 5.25 percent with a neutral stance in early July 2026. RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra projected real GDP growth of 6.9 percent for the current fiscal year, down from 7.6 percent in the previous year. CPI inflation is expected to hold at 4.6 percent, with oil prices and potential El Niño conditions posing upside risks. The standing deposit facility rate remains at 5 percent, while the marginal standing facility rate stays at 5.5 percent.

**Why it matters.** Founders burning through capital will face a sustained lending environment at 5.25 percent—not declining further soon. A 6.9 percent GDP growth projection suggests continued economic headwinds that could dampen enterprise technology spending and lengthen sales cycles for B2B AI tools and fintech platforms. For consultants and contractors, this reinforces the macro trend: conservative capital deployment, longer time-to-profitability. The neutral stance means rate hikes are possible if inflation moves higher, tightening funding access further. Angel investors and venture debt lenders will price deals accordingly.

**Action.** Pull RBI's full monetary policy statement from rbi.org.in to review guidance on credit growth and inflation trajectory. Track the next MPC meeting scheduled for August to see if growth slowdown triggers any pivots. Review your burn rate and runway assuming no additional capital infusions at current deployment costs.

*Sources: [[RBI Monetary Policy Statement](https://www.rbi.org.in/scripts/annualpolicy.aspx)]([RBI Monetary Policy Statement](https://www.rbi.org.in/scripts/annualpolicy.aspx)) · [[Akashvani News Coverage](https://newsonair.gov.in/rbi-to-announce-its-first-bi-monthly-monetary-policy-statement-for-financial-year-2026-27-today/)]([Akashvani News Coverage](https://newsonair.gov.in/rbi-to-announce-its-first-bi-monthly-monetary-policy-statement-for-financial-year-2026-27-today/))*

---

### Indian AI Startups Surge to $676M in H1 2026—4X Growth, But Concentration Deepens

Indian AI startups raised $676 million across 57 funding deals in the first half of 2026, marking a 4X increase from $162 million across 30 deals in H1 2025. The spike reflects a broader H1 2026 trend: while total Indian startup funding fell 43 percent by deal count to just $5.2 billion, AI, fintech, and cleantech companies attracted disproportionately larger cheques as venture capital consolidated around well-capitalized, technology-heavy founders. The IndiaAI Mission, backed by ₹10,000 crore in government compute and infrastructure, has onboarded 38,000 GPUs to an affordable public portal for startups and academia, with Sarvam AI, Gnani.ai, Gan.ai, and Souket.ai selected for foundational model development.

**Why it matters.** The AI funding concentration tells you where the market is moving—consolidation around technical depth, not breadth. If you're building AI applied to Indian verticals (fintech, logistics, commerce, healthcare), you're in the heat-map. Government compute access via IndiaAI Mission GPU allocations is now a material advantage: it reduces capex friction for model fine-tuning and inference serving. But the inverse is real: non-AI startups without a clear AI angle are starved of capital. For those switching from consulting into product roles, this is the moment—VC appetite for AI founders is at its highest, and government infrastructure removes bottlenecks around GPU access that plagued 2025 founders.

**Action.** Register on the IndiaAI compute portal (indiaai.gov.in) to assess GPU availability and pricing for your workloads. Review H1 2026 funding data via Tracxn or Inc42 to see which AI application areas (fintech, supply chain, document automation, medical imaging) received the most capital—track those founder decks for product patterns. Check eligibility for the IndiaAI Innovation Challenge 2026 if you have a deployed AI solution ready to scale.

*Sources: [[Inc42 AI Funding Report H1 2026](https://inc42.com/features/indian-ai-startup-funding-soars-over-4x-yoy-in-h1-2026-but-is-it-enough-to-compete-globally/)]([Inc42 AI Funding Report H1 2026](https://inc42.com/features/indian-ai-startup-funding-soars-over-4x-yoy-in-h1-2026-but-is-it-enough-to-compete-globally/)) · [[IndiaAI Mission Compute Portal](https://indiaai.gov.in/hub/indiaai-startup-financing)]([IndiaAI Mission Compute Portal](https://indiaai.gov.in/hub/indiaai-startup-financing)) · [[Tracxn Deep Tech Data](https://tracxn.com/d/explore/deep-tech-startups-in-india/)]([Tracxn Deep Tech Data](https://tracxn.com/d/explore/deep-tech-startups-in-india/))*

## 📈 Markets

### Fed Pivot Bets Lift India Tech While RBI Stays Hawkish on Inflation

Nifty 50 closed at 24,270.85, up 95.15 points (0.39%), with Sensex gaining 261.79 points (0.34%) to 77,763.91 on July 3 as softer US payrolls data resurrected Fed easing expectations. Nifty IT surged 1.8%, its best single-day gain in 14 months, as beaten-down tech stocks attracted fresh capital on the view that dollar weakness and lower US rates would lift global liquidity. Meanwhile, the rupee held firm at ₹95.22 per dollar, India's 10-year G-Sec yield compressed to 6.71%, and the US 10-year Treasury stayed anchored at 4.48%, signaling a shift in rate cycle expectations after the Fed held at 3.5-3.75% in June.

**Why it matters.** The RBI's June decision to hold the repo at 5.25% while raising inflation forecasts to 5.1% (from 4.6%) creates a macro divergence with the Fed. India faces structural inflation pressures from crude (still at $72/barrel), supply shocks, and monsoon uncertainty—constraints that limit RBI easing even if global rates fall. For quant prop shops watching carry and cross-asset correlation, this bifurcation matters: Indian equities (especially tech exports) benefit from USD depreciation and lower US rates, but the carry advantage of INR picks up only if rupee weakness outpaces rate-cut flows into India. The rising inflation forecast (now 5.1% from 4.6%, with Q3-Q4 inflation tipping 5.9%) signals the RBI sees persistent headwinds—geopolitical oil shocks, El Niño weather, West Asia conflict spillovers. This constrains the RBI's policy space and keeps real rates sticky, locking in the beta of Indian equities to global growth and CapEx cycles rather than monetary stimulus.

**Action.** Watch the Fed's July 28-29 FOMC outcome for tone on timing of cuts—equity beta to that decision is now fully priced into Nifty IT. Track crude oil and the rupee: if Brent stays below $75 and INR stays above ₹95.50, the RBI's July MPC might signal easing odds in 2H. Key prints ahead: India's June inflation print (expected Q2 FY27 CPI and WPI data mid-July) and US PCE on July 9 to calibrate the Fed's September risk appetite.

*Sources: [[Stock Market Close Report, July 3, 2026](https://hdfcsky.com/news/stock-market-close-report-july-3-2026-sensex-nifty-extend-winning-streak-as-fed-fears-recede)]([Stock Market Close Report, July 3, 2026](https://hdfcsky.com/news/stock-market-close-report-july-3-2026-sensex-nifty-extend-winning-streak-as-fed-fears-recede)) · [[RBI MPC June 2026 highlights](https://www.forbesindia.com/article/forbesindiablogs/rbi-mpc-june-2026-live-repo-rate-decision-governor-sanjay-malhotra-gdp-data-updates-liveblog/2994645/1)]([RBI MPC June 2026 highlights](https://www.forbesindia.com/article/forbesindiablogs/rbi-mpc-june-2026-live-repo-rate-decision-governor-sanjay-malhotra-gdp-data-updates-liveblog/2994645/1)) · [[US Treasury Yield Data](https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DGS10)]([US Treasury Yield Data](https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DGS10))*

### Gold Hits 3-Week Peak While Crude Stabilizes—Commodity Bifurcation Signals Contained Geopolitical Spillover

Gold climbed to $4,170 per ounce (₹13,450 per gram) on July 4, touching its highest level since June 23 amid risk repricing and Fed easing bets, while Brent crude remained anchored in the $71.94–$72.50 range. The dollar's weakness on softer US labor expectations sent flows into safe havens, but crude failed to breach $75 despite the West Asia conflict backdrop and OPEC+ supply considerations. The asymmetric move—gold strength unmatched by oil—suggests geopolitical risk is being priced as contained and temporary rather than a sustained structural shock to energy markets.

**Why it matters.** For props trading commodities spreads, the gold-to-crude ratio divergence reveals market consensus that oil remains vulnerable to demand destruction (especially if Fed cuts happen), while precious metals benefit from both rate cuts and fiat debasement hedging. India's inflation forecast of 5.1% (with Q3 tipping 5.9%) makes crude the binding constraint on RBI easing, not rupee strength or external factors. A stable crude at $72 removes the RBI's primary inflation excuse, potentially opening the door to rate cuts in September-October if global disinflation accelerates. For Citadel-type models, the gold rally off lower real yields (US 10Y at 4.48%, real yields in the 2.0-2.2% range given inflation expectations) is synchronized with equities, not decorrelated—a signal that the "volmageddon short" (betting on correlations staying low) could face pinch-points if rate cuts accelerate faster than growth reprices.

**Action.** Monitor crude above $73.50—a break signals OPEC+ production cuts or fresh geopolitical escalation taking hold in markets. Track the gold futures curve (contango vs. backwardation) to see if safe-haven flows are structural or mean-reversion. Watch the rupee if crude spikes: INR above ₹96 and crude above $75 simultaneously would force the RBI to pause easing prematurely, creating a tactical trade for commodities baskets versus Indian equity momentum.

*Sources: [[Gold Rate Today](https://www.goodreturns.in/gold-rates/)]([Gold Rate Today](https://www.goodreturns.in/gold-rates/)) · [[Trading Economics Commodity Data](https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/gold)]([Trading Economics Commodity Data](https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/gold)) · [[RBI Inflation Forecast June 2026](https://www.forbesindia.com/article/forbesindiablogs/rbi-mpc-june-2026-live-repo-rate-decision-governor-sanjay-malhotra-gdp-data-updates-liveblog/2994645/1)]([RBI Inflation Forecast June 2026](https://www.forbesindia.com/article/forbesindiablogs/rbi-mpc-june-2026-live-repo-rate-decision-governor-sanjay-malhotra-gdp-data-updates-liveblog/2994645/1))*

## 💹 Global Stocks

### TSM (NYSE): +4.94% on Mizuho capacity upgrade, BofA raises price target to $590

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing closed at $477.57 on July 2, up $22.47 or 4.94% on heavy institutional demand. Mizuho Securities raised capacity forecasts for TSMC's CoWoS advanced packaging technology amid strong server CPU demand for AI inferencing. Bank of America Securities raised its price target from $490 to $590, citing record AI earnings and a valuation that "remains cheap" despite year-to-date gains of 60%.

**Why it matters.** CoWoS is the critical technology bottleneck for GPU packaging in the AI infrastructure buildout. Mizuho's capacity raise signals confidence that the supply constraint easing will accelerate TSMC's 2026 cash generation. The stock's move reflects a structural repricing of AI compute availability — if TSMC can deliver 3-4x throughput on advanced packaging by 2027, it directly alleviates NVIDIA/AMD/AVGO's ability to ship volume at scale, and sends a signal to India's semiconductor ambitions that the global race is moving into mature manufacturing phases.

**Action.** File 6-K (most recent quarterly filing) for capacity CapEx guidance; monitor Nasdaq quote for $500 technical resistance; track BofA equity research updates on TSMC cross-holdings (NVDA, AVGO exposure). CoWoS waitlist status appears as a key metric in TSM's next investor day.

*Sources: [finance.yahoo.com](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TSM/) · [am.jpmorgan.com](https://am.jpmorgan.com/content/dam/jpm-am-aem/global/en/insights/market-insights/guide-to-the-markets/guide-to-the-markets-asia.pdf) · [bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/graphics/2026-samsung-tsmc-ai-rally/)*

---

### GOOGL (NASDAQ): -0.36% as EU antitrust ruling hits €4.1 billion, Sweden adds $2 billion liability

Alphabet closed at $359.91 on July 2, down $1.30 or 0.36%, as European regulatory bodies tightened the screws on ad-tech practices. The EU imposed a €4.1 billion antitrust ruling on Google's search and advertising business, while a separate Swedish court ordered the company to pay approximately $2 billion related to search monopoly practices. Despite antitrust headwinds, the company launched new competitive AI models to offset OpenAI and Claude market share losses.

**Why it matters.** Google faces a two-track regulatory squeeze — each $1-2 billion ruling is individually manageable but signals a pattern: EU regulators are now extracting structural penalties on search moat leverage, not just fines. The Sweden ruling suggests US regulatory bodies may follow suit by Q4 2026. For investors, the calculus shifts: Alphabet's core search business now carries regulatory overhang that reduces free cash flow estimates by 5-7% annually. The AI model launches indicate management is hedging against long-term search cannibalization by Cloud/Gemini revenue, a read-across to MSFT's identical strategy and AMZN's underinvestment in foundation models.

**Action.** File 8-K for material legal contingencies; monitor 10-Q MD&A for accruals tied to pending EU/UK investigations; track antitrust.org and MLex filings for DOJ Search case timeline updates. Next catalyst: Q2 earnings call (likely week of July 20) will reveal guidance impact from regulatory charges.

*Sources: [finance.yahoo.com](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/GOOGL/) · [https://www.jpmorganasset management.com/en/insights/market-insights/guide-to-the-markets/guide-to-the-markets-asia.pdf](https://www.jpmorganasset management.com/en/insights/market-insights/guide-to-the-markets/guide-to-the-markets-asia.pdf) · [asia.nikkei.com](https://asia.nikkei.com/business/markets/asia-market-snapshot-of-best-and-worst-performers-in-2026-first-half)*

## 🏗 Tech & Startups

### Bending Spoons Goes Public, Jumps 40% as Italian SaaS Acquirer Breaks $25 Billion Market Cap

Bending Spoons, a Milan-based software company that has built a portfolio of 50+ internet brands, went public on Nasdaq July 1 at $29 per share, raising $1.68 billion. The stock surged 40% on its first trading day to close at $40.50, valuing the company at $25.7 billion—more than double its $11 billion private valuation from late 2025. Founded in 2013 by Italian college classmates Matteo Danieli, Luca Ferrari, Francesco Patarnello, and Luca Querella, Bending Spoons owns Vimeo, Eventbrite, Meetup, WeTransfer, AOL, and Evernote.

**Why it matters.** Bending Spoons is proving that acquiring and operationally transforming struggling SaaS companies at scale works as a public market bet. While traditional SaaS grinds through a valuation crunch, this company is buying beloved brands at distressed multiples and executing private-equity-style operational efficiency—revenue per employee jumped from $1.12M in 2023 to $2.57M in 2025. For builders, the signal is clear: defensible brands with loyal user bases have real value even if their standalone business models are broken. The IPO pop suggests investors believe Bending Spoons can continue this roll-up playbook at public-market scale.

**Action.** Track how Bending Spoons deploys its fresh $1.68B in capital—acquisitions or organic growth is the next test. Founders running "best-of-breed" SaaS tools should study how it has restored profitability to Vimeo and Eventbrite; the playbook (product investment + operational tightening) is worth copying regardless of your acquisition risk.

*Sources: [techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/01/after-18b-ipo-bending-spoons-founder-says-success-comes-from-minimizing-luck/) · [bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-01/bending-spoons-ipo-turns-italian-college-pals-into-billionaires) · [techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/01/bending-spoons-defies-saas-slump-surges-40-on-first-day-of-trading/)*

### Canada's Defence Play: Dominion Dynamics Raises $100M Series A for Arctic Surveillance and Autonomous Drones

Dominion Dynamics, an Ottawa-based defence technology startup, closed a C$139 million ($100 million USD) Series A on June 30, 2026, led by Georgian and backed by Valor Equity Partners, OMERS, Lakestar, BDC, and others. The round marks the largest Series A in Canadian defence history. Dominion has already deployed its AuraNet command-and-control platform with the Canadian Armed Forces during Arctic operations and is scaling production of Scout, an autonomous drone platform designed to extend fighter jet capabilities into remote, extreme environments.

**Why it matters.** Defence tech is capturing venture capital at record pace as geopolitical tension drives equipment spending. Dominion's Series A signals that venture capitalists are betting on Canadian industrial capacity to build next-generation defence systems—not just buying off-the-shelf. The startup is solving a real problem: Arctic operations are brutal, comms are unreliable, and manned jets can't go everywhere. The AuraNet platform (already battle-tested with Canadian Rangers) aggregates scattered sensor data into a single operational picture. For founders in defence, the message is execution + proof of concept (Dominion had already deployed and shown value) unlocks capital even at $100M+.

**Action.** Watch how Dominion scales manufacturing at its new 25,000-square-foot Kanata facility—defence procurement cycles are slow, but if Canadian procurement accelerates (as leadership suggests), early suppliers will lock in long-term contracts. Founders building for extreme environments (Arctic, undersea, space) should note that this sector is drawing institutional capital without requiring venture-scale unit economics.

*Sources: [newswire.ca](https://www.newswire.ca/news-releases/dominion-dynamics-raises-139m-cad-series-a-to-scale-arctic-surveillance-network-and-drone-systems-859179965.html) · [betakit.com](https://betakit.com/dominion-dynamics-lands-139-million-in-canadas-largest-defence-tech-series-a) · [spaceq.ca](https://spaceq.ca/dominion-dynamics-closes-139m-series-a-for-arctic-and-space-defence-systems/)*

## 🩹 Health & Science

### Phase 3 Trial Shows Relacorilant Cuts Ovarian Cancer Deaths by 35 Percent

The ROSELLA trial enrolled 381 patients with platinum-resistant ovarian cancer across 15 countries and randomly assigned them to receive either relacorilant (a first-in-class glucocorticoid receptor antagonist) plus nab-paclitaxel chemotherapy, or nab-paclitaxel alone. Patients receiving relacorilant achieved median overall survival of 16.0 months versus 11.9 months in the control arm, representing a 35% reduction in death risk (hazard ratio 0.65; p=0.0004). The trial met its dual primary endpoints of progression-free survival and overall survival.

**Why it matters.** Platinum-resistant ovarian cancer has seen minimal survival progress over two decades—this is the first major OS improvement in this setting, offering a new mechanism (glucocorticoid antagonism) that restores chemotherapy sensitivity in a population with severely limited options. The effect size is clinically meaningful: a 4.1-month median OS improvement in a disease that kills within 12 months. For builders in oncology AI and biomarker discovery, this validates the glucocorticoid stress-response pathway as actionable, opening a new category for patient stratification and combination studies.

**Action.** Read the ROSELLA Phase 3 results published in The Lancet June 2026. The mechanism offers a template for stress-response-targeted combinations in other chemoresistant cancers. Oncology programs should validate predictive biomarkers for patient selection; investors should explore downstream combinations with checkpoint inhibitors or other chemotherapy partners.

*Sources: [[The Lancet: Overall survival with relacorilant and nab-paclitaxel in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer](https://www.thelancet.com/journals/lancet/article/PIIS0140-6736(26)00462-9/fulltext)]([The Lancet: Overall survival with relacorilant and nab-paclitaxel in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer](https://www.thelancet.com/journals/lancet/article/PIIS0140-6736(26)00462-9/fulltext)) · [[Corcept Press Release](https://ir.corcept.com/news-releases/news-release-details/overall-survival-primary-endpoint-met-corcepts-pivotal-phase-3/)]([Corcept Press Release](https://ir.corcept.com/news-releases/news-release-details/overall-survival-primary-endpoint-met-corcepts-pivotal-phase-3/)) · [[OncLive Coverage](https://www.onclive.com/view/relacorilant-plus-nab-paclitaxel-significantly-improves-os-in-platinum-resistant-ovarian-cancer)]([OncLive Coverage](https://www.onclive.com/view/relacorilant-plus-nab-paclitaxel-significantly-improves-os-in-platinum-resistant-ovarian-cancer))*

### Harvard Study Maps Longevity Dose for Strength Training: 90–120 Minutes Per Week

Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health followed 147,000 adults for 30 years and found that 90–120 minutes of weekly strength training reduces all-cause mortality by 13%, cardiovascular death by 19%, and neurological death by 27%. The optimal dose plateaued at 120 minutes; additional training beyond that threshold yielded no incremental mortality benefit. Results were published in the British Journal of Sports Medicine on June 2, 2026.

**Why it matters.** This is one of the largest long-term datasets quantifying strength training's dose-response relationship and moving beyond "exercise is good" to a precise, actionable prescription. The finding that more is not better challenges conventional fitness messaging and enables targeted public health recommendations. For health tech builders, this validates strength training as a primary longevity lever comparable to cardio, unlocking product and content opportunities around dose optimization and adherence tracking.

**Action.** Implement 90–120 minutes of structured resistance training weekly (roughly two to three focused sessions). Combine with aerobic activity for additive protection. For researchers: validate whether predictive biomarkers (metabolic age, muscle composition, grip strength) can identify responders versus non-responders. For builders: design adherence tools that lock users into this dose-response window and track mortality surrogates.

*Sources: [[British Journal of Sports Medicine: Strength training and all-cause mortality, cardiovascular disease, and neurological mortality](https://hsph.harvard.edu/news/moderate-amount-of-strength-training-each-week-could-boost-longevity/)]([British Journal of Sports Medicine: Strength training and all-cause mortality, cardiovascular disease, and neurological mortality](https://hsph.harvard.edu/news/moderate-amount-of-strength-training-each-week-could-boost-longevity/)) · [[ScienceDaily Coverage](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/06/260611024609.htm)]([ScienceDaily Coverage](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/06/260611024609.htm)) · [[Medical Daily](https://www.medicaldaily.com/strength-training-longevity-sweet-spot-harvard-30-year-study-2026-475633)]([Medical Daily](https://www.medicaldaily.com/strength-training-longevity-sweet-spot-harvard-30-year-study-2026-475633))*

## 🎤 Culture & World

### Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey Premieres in London, Mythic Epic Arrives July 17

Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey premiered in London on July 6, 2026, ahead of its July 17 theatrical release in the US and UK. The film chronicles Odysseus's journey home following the Trojan War, featuring encounters with the Cyclops, Sirens, and Calypso. Nolan releases the film in IMAX and premium large formats, marking his first foray into classical mythology as source material.

**Why it matters.** Nolan's pivot to Homer signals major directors are still betting on theatrical spectacle with literary prestige—not IP or franchise material. The mythology angle is a cultural inflection point: audiences rotate toward classical narratives when contemporary ones feel depleted. For Shashank's distribution lens, this is Nolan proving auteur-scale budgets work outside the Marvel orbit, while the IMAX push anchors the theatrical experience as distinct from streaming.

**Action.** Watch the opening weekend box office against Nolan's historical pulls. The runtime and depth will tell you whether audiences show up for 3-hour mythic storytelling, or whether even Nolan's brand can't sustain that commitment in 2026. Stream availability later will reveal whether Disney/Universal see this as tentpole momentum or catalog depth.

*Sources: [[Wikipedia: The Odyssey (2026 film)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Odyssey_(2026_film))]([Wikipedia: The Odyssey (2026 film)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Odyssey_(2026_film))) · [[Rotten Tomatoes: Everything We Know About The Odyssey](https://editorial.rottentomatoes.com/article/everything-we-know-about-the-odyssey/)]([Rotten Tomatoes: Everything We Know About The Odyssey](https://editorial.rottentomatoes.com/article/everything-we-know-about-the-odyssey/)) · [[Odyssey Movie Site](https://www.odysseymovie.com/)]([Odyssey Movie Site](https://www.odysseymovie.com/))*

### Oasis Reunion Documentary Teaser Arrives; Don't Look Back in Anger Hits Theaters September 11

On July 4—one year after Oasis played their first reunion show in Cardiff—the band released a teaser for Don't Look Back in Anger, a Steven Knight–directed documentary following the 2025 reunion tour. The film captures rehearsal, backstage, and onstage footage, plus the brothers' first joint interview in over 20 years. It arrives in limited IMAX theaters September 11 before moving to Disney+ and Hulu.

**Why it matters.** The Oasis reunion was arguably the biggest live music event of 2025; the documentary strategy is two-fold cultural signal. First, nostalgia and reconciliation drives audiences to theaters (Gallagher brothers reuniting after 16 years). Second, Disney's staged rollout—limited theatrical window, then streaming—is the 2026 playbook for music docs: theatrical prestige first, then mass reach. For Shashank, this shows how legacy acts still move the distribution needle, and how streaming services use them to anchor quarterly subscriber narratives.

**Action.** Track the September 11 box office as a referendum on whether music documentaries can justify theatrical spending. Watch Twitter/Threads discourse around Noel and Liam's interview—40+ years of Oasis tension distilled into one sit-down is catnip for music criticism and meme culture. Monitor Disney+ subscriber lift in September.

*Sources: [[NME: First Trailer for Steven Knight's Oasis Documentary](https://www.nme.com/news/music/oasis-documentary-trailer-steven-knight-watch-3955023)]([NME: First Trailer for Steven Knight's Oasis Documentary](https://www.nme.com/news/music/oasis-documentary-trailer-steven-knight-watch-3955023)) · [[Deadline: Don't Look Back in Anger Teaser Follows Oasis Reunion Tour](https://deadline.com/2026/07/dont-look-back-in-anger-teaser-oasis-reunion-tour-1236974261/)]([Deadline: Don't Look Back in Anger Teaser Follows Oasis Reunion Tour](https://deadline.com/2026/07/dont-look-back-in-anger-teaser-oasis-reunion-tour-1236974261/)) · [[Yahoo News UK: Oasis Release Teaser for Tour Documentary](https://uk.news.yahoo.com/oasis-release-teaser-tour-documentary-010713363.html)]([Yahoo News UK: Oasis Release Teaser for Tour Documentary](https://uk.news.yahoo.com/oasis-release-teaser-tour-documentary-010713363.html))*
